2026-04-08T10:18:22.0447248Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-08T10:18:22.0447700Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-08T10:18:22.0448065Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-08T10:18:22.0448379Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m

Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"issue_number":{"description":"Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"state_reason":{"description":"The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.","enum":["COMPLETED","NOT_PLANNED","DUPLICATE"],"type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"close_issue"},{"description":"Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_comment"},{"description":"Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. 2026-04-08T10:21:23.2876628Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-08T10:19:28.459403303Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57931,\"title\":\"[Management Generator] Docs: Document extension resource detection with specific parent types\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt`\\n**Service directory:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/`\\n**Triggered by:** Commit [REDACTED] (PR #57722) by `@live1206`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #57722 fixed a bug in the management generator\\u0026#39;s resource detection logic to correctly handle **extension resources that target a specific parent resource type** (e.g., a resource that can only extend `Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines` rather than any arbitrary resource). The fix touched `resource-detection.ts`, `resource-metadata.ts`, and `resolve-arm-resources-converter.ts`.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The existing `docs/OperationContext.md` covers extension resources only for the **generic/wildcard scope** case (paths starting with `/{resourceUri}/providers/...`), but does not explain extension resources that specify a **specific parent resource type** via the TypeSpec `@armResourceOperations` scope, nor how the generator detects and differentiates between the two\\n- There is no documentation explaining **how the generator detects whether an extension resource has a specific parent type vs. a wildcard scope**, and what generated code differs between the two cases (e.g., which extension class the resource\\u0026#39;s helper methods are generated on)\\n- No CHANGELOG file exists in `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/` to record this bug fix for users of the generator who may have been working around it\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File 1: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/OperationContext.md` — **edit**\\n\\nExpand the existing **Extension Resources** subsection (under **Advanced Scenarios**) to cover the specific parent type case. Replace the current section:\\n\\n````markdown\\n### Extension Resources\\n\\nExtension resources have a flexible scope (can extend any resource). Their contextual path starts with a single variable segment:\\n```\\n/{resourceUri}/providers/Microsoft.Example/extensions/{extensionName}\\n```\\n\\nThe system handles these specially, treating `resourceUri` as a contextual parameter derived from the parent resource\\u0026#39;s Id.\\n````\\n\\nwith:\\n\\n````markdown\\n### Extension Resources\\n\\nExtension resources extend another Azure resource. The generator recognizes two sub-types:\\n\\n#### Wildcard-scope extension resources\\n\\nWhen an extension resource can extend **any** resource type, the operation paths use a single variable prefix:\\n\\n```\\n/{resourceUri}/providers/Microsoft.Example/extensions/{extensionName}\\n```\\n\\nThe system treats `resourceUri` as a contextual parameter derived from the extended resource\\u0026#39;s `Id`. Helper methods are added to the `ArmClient` mockable extension class so callers can invoke them from any `ArmResource`.\\n\\n#### Specific-parent extension resources\\n\\nWhen an extension resource is scoped to a **specific parent resource type** (for example, it can only extend `Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines`), the TypeSpec definition constrains the scope. The detected parent type determines which Azure scope class (e.g., `ResourceGroupResource`, `SubscriptionResource`, `TenantResource`, `ManagementGroupResource`, or the parent resource\\u0026#39;s own collection/resource class) receives the helper extension methods.\\n\\nThe generator distinguishes these cases during resource detection by inspecting whether the extension resource\\u0026#39;s path prefix resolves to a known Azure scope path rather than a generic variable segment. When a specific parent is detected, the resource\\u0026#39;s `parentResourceId` in the code model is set to the concrete parent path, enabling the generator to place helper methods on the correct extension class.\\n\\n**Example:** An extension resource with parent type `Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines`:\\n\\n```\\n/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/{vmName}/providers/Microsoft.Example/extensions/{extensionName}\\n```\\n\\nThe generator detects `Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines` as the specific parent and adds `GetExtension(string extensionName)` to the `MockableVirtualMachineResource` class, not the generic `ArmClient` extension.\\n````\\n\\n#### File 2: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/CHANGELOG.md` — **create**\\n\\nCreate a CHANGELOG file to track generator releases and bug fixes:\\n\\n```markdown\\n# Release History\\n\\n## Unreleased\\n\\n### Bug Fixes\\n\\n- Fixed detection of extension resources that target a specific parent resource type. Previously, extension resources with a constrained scope (e.g., restricted to `Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines`) were not correctly identified as having a specific parent, causing helper methods to be generated on the wrong extension class or missing entirely. ([#57722](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/pull/57722))\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo README snippet changes are needed for this developer-tool documentation update. The docs are prose-only markdown inside `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/`.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md` is a generator tool README, not an end-user SDK README, so the standard template sections do not strictly apply. However, consider adding a **\\u0026#34;Known Limitations / Notes\\u0026#34;** or a link to the CHANGELOG once it exists.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nThis is a documentation-only change (markdown files and a new CHANGELOG). No build or test commands are required beyond a basic review. However, to confirm the generator itself still works:\\n\\n1. `cd eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt \\u0026amp;\\u0026amp; npm install`\\n2. `./eng/scripts/Generate.ps1` — re-generates all test projects; should complete with no errors\\n3. Review `git diff` on the generated test project files to confirm no regressions\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24129939236/agentic_workflow) · ● 472.1K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22\\u0026amp;type=issues)\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57931\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"github-actions[bot]\",\"id\":41898282,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/apps/github-actions\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":0,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":0},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-08T10:16:29Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-08T10:16:29Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}
2026-04-08T10:21:23.2902806Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-08T10:19:28.474012278Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"github","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"issue_number":57931,"method":"get","owner":"Azure","repo":"azure-sdk-for-net"},"name":"issue_read"}}}
2026-04-08T10:21:23.2933440Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-08T10:19:28.726002473Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57931,\"title\":\"[Management Generator] Docs: Document extension resource detection with specific parent types\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt`\\n**Service directory:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/`\\n**Triggered by:** Commit [REDACTED] (PR #57722) by `@live1206`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #57722 fixed a bug in the management generator\\u0026#39;s resource detection logic to correctly handle **extension resources that target a specific parent resource type** (e.g., a resource that can only extend `Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines` rather than any arbitrary resource). The fix touched `resource-detection.ts`, `resource-metadata.ts`, and `resolve-arm-resources-converter.ts`.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The existing `docs/OperationContext.md` covers extension resources only for the **generic/wildcard scope** case (paths starting with `/{resourceUri}/providers/...`), but does not explain extension resources that specify a **specific parent resource type** via the TypeSpec `@armResourceOperations` scope, nor how the generator detects and differentiates between the two\\n- There is no documentation explaining **how the generator detects whether an extension resource has a specific parent type vs. a wildcard scope**, and what generated code differs between the two cases (e.g., which extension class the resource\\u0026#39;s helper methods are generated on)\\n- No CHANGELOG file exists in `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/` to record this bug fix for users of the generator who may have been working around it\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File 1: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/OperationContext.md` — **edit**\\n\\nExpand the existing **Extension Resources** subsection (under **Advanced Scenarios**) to cover the specific parent type case. Replace the current section:\\n\\n````markdown\\n### Extension Resources\\n\\nExtension resources have a flexible scope (can extend any resource). Their contextual path starts with a single variable segment:\\n```\\n/{resourceUri}/providers/Microsoft.Example/extensions/{extensionName}\\n```\\n\\nThe system handles these specially, treating `resourceUri` as a contextual parameter derived from the parent resource\\u0026#39;s Id.\\n````\\n\\nwith:\\n\\n````markdown\\n### Extension Resources\\n\\nExtension resources extend another Azure resource. The generator recognizes two sub-types:\\n\\n#### Wildcard-scope extension resources\\n\\nWhen an extension resource can extend **any** resource type, the operation paths use a single variable prefix:\\n\\n```\\n/{resourceUri}/providers/Microsoft.Example/extensions/{extensionName}\\n```\\n\\nThe system treats `resourceUri` as a contextual parameter derived from the extended resource\\u0026#39;s `Id`. Helper methods are added to the `ArmClient` mockable extension class so callers can invoke them from any `ArmResource`.\\n\\n#### Specific-parent extension resources\\n\\nWhen an extension resource is scoped to a **specific parent resource type** (for example, it can only extend `Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines`), the TypeSpec definition constrains the scope. The detected parent type determines which Azure scope class (e.g., `ResourceGroupResource`, `SubscriptionResource`, `TenantResource`, `ManagementGroupResource`, or the parent resource\\u0026#39;s own collection/resource class) receives the helper extension methods.\\n\\nThe generator distinguishes these cases during resource detection by inspecting whether the extension resource\\u0026#39;s path prefix resolves to a known Azure scope path rather than a generic variable segment. When a specific parent is detected, the resource\\u0026#39;s `parentResourceId` in the code model is set to the concrete parent path, enabling the generator to place helper methods on the correct extension class.\\n\\n**Example:** An extension resource with parent type `Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines`:\\n\\n```\\n/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/{vmName}/providers/Microsoft.Example/extensions/{extensionName}\\n```\\n\\nThe generator detects `Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines` as the specific parent and adds `GetExtension(string extensionName)` to the `MockableVirtualMachineResource` class, not the generic `ArmClient` extension.\\n````\\n\\n#### File 2: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/CHANGELOG.md` — **create**\\n\\nCreate a CHANGELOG file to track generator releases and bug fixes:\\n\\n```markdown\\n# Release History\\n\\n## Unreleased\\n\\n### Bug Fixes\\n\\n- Fixed detection of extension resources that target a specific parent resource type. Previously, extension resources with a constrained scope (e.g., restricted to `Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines`) were not correctly identified as having a specific parent, causing helper methods to be generated on the wrong extension class or missing entirely. ([#57722](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/pull/57722))\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo README snippet changes are needed for this developer-tool documentation update. The docs are prose-only markdown inside `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/`.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md` is a generator tool README, not an end-user SDK README, so the standard template sections do not strictly apply. However, consider adding a **\\u0026#34;Known Limitations / Notes\\u0026#34;** or a link to the CHANGELOG once it exists.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nThis is a documentation-only change (markdown files and a new CHANGELOG). No build or test commands are required beyond a basic review. However, to confirm the generator itself still works:\\n\\n1. `cd eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt \\u0026amp;\\u0026amp; npm install`\\n2. `./eng/scripts/Generate.ps1` — re-generates all test projects; should complete with no errors\\n3. Review `git diff` on the generated test project files to confirm no regressions\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24129939236/agentic_workflow) · ● 472.1K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22\\u0026amp;type=issues)\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57931\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"github-actions[bot]\",\"id\":41898282,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/apps/github-actions\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":0,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":0},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-08T10:16:29Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-08T10:16:29Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}
